title: Enhancing Protein Sequence Pattern Discovery with Recurrent Neural Networks and the Megaprior Heuristic
abstract: The discovery of patterns in protein sequences is crucial for understanding biological functions and evolutionary relationships. Traditional methods, such as Hidden Markov Models (HMMs) and motif discovery algorithms, often face challenges with convex combinations, leading to inaccurate models. This paper explores the integration of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) with the megaprior heuristic to address this issue. We propose a novel framework that leverages the strengths of RNNs in capturing sequential dependencies and the megaprior heuristic in reducing variance in prior distributions. Our methodology involves training RNNs on protein sequence datasets with extremely low variance Dirichlet mixture priors. We analyze the performance of this integrated approach through extensive experiments on diverse protein families. Results demonstrate a significant reduction in convex combination errors and improved accuracy in pattern discovery. This study underscores the potential of combining advanced machine learning techniques with statistical heuristics for more robust protein sequence analysis.
